title: Schizoloma erectum
intro: Schizoloma erectum, also known as Horsetail or Equisetum, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the Equisetaceae family and is often mistaken as a fern or moss due to its unique appearance. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as supporting cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Schizoloma erectum include flavonoids, saponins, and silica, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Schizoloma erectum is used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, including arthritis, gout, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Schizoloma erectum is classified as a species of fern within the family Aspleniaceae. This family is a group of small to medium-sized ferns commonly known as spleenworts. Schizoloma erectum is specifically classified within the genus Schizoloma, which is a genus of ferns native to Europe. The genus Schizoloma is further classified within the order Aspleniineae, which is a group of ferns that are typically small to medium in size. The full classification for Schizoloma erectum is Schizoloma erectum (Lam.) Roth, which includes its genus, species, and author credit.
